content_hints.description: South Soffit, after midnight. Rain slicks the cobblestones and turns the gaslamp light into smeared amber puddles. A narrow alley between a pawnbroker and a closed butcher's shop. A detective in a rain-darkened overcoat runs, his footsteps splashing. He is chasing something -- or being chased by it. A shadow moves on the rooftop above, too large, too many limbs.
content_hints.read_aloud: A gunshot cracks the rain-silence. Then another. The detective stumbles out of the alley, clutching his right hand to his chest. His right hand is gone -- severed at the wrist, the stump cauterized by something that is not heat. Silk threads trail from the wound, gossamer-thin, connecting his severed hand to the shadow on the rooftop above. Spiders pour from the roofline. Thousands of them. They swarm the detective's body in a chitinous wave, and when they disperse thirty seconds later, the body has no face.
event_triggers: A private detective is killed in South Soffit. His face is removed by a swarm of spiders. His severed hand is connected to the rooftop by silk threads. Candela Obscura is notified through irregular channels.
The detective's name is Miles Crane. He was hired to find a missing person. He found something else.

content_hints.description: The crime scene, minutes later. The rain is washing away the blood. Spider silk threads glisten on every surface -- lampposts, gutters, window ledges -- forming a web that radiates outward from the alley. The detective's body lies face-down in a puddle. When you turn it over, the face is simply gone -- not torn, not eaten, but removed. Smooth skin where features should be. A business card protrudes from his breast pocket.
event_triggers: The spider silk is not natural. Under magnification, each strand contains a faint bioluminescent core that pulses with bleed energy. The web is a sensory network -- whatever built it can feel vibrations across the entire district.

loot.name: Miles Crane's Business Card
loot.description: Front: 'Miles Crane, Private Investigations. Discretion Guaranteed.' Back, handwritten in pencil: 'Florence Brighton -- Eden?' A lead. Florence and Brighton are cross-streets in South Soffit. The Eden Theater sits at their intersection.
